assertions.assertion: Code uses ISessionManagementService with QuerySessionsAsync and CountRequested = 10 for the first page
assertions.rationale: ISessionManagementService and its QuerySessionsAsync method with CountRequested property are Duende-specific APIs unlikely to be known without docs.

assertions.assertion: Code uses ResultsToken from the first query result to fetch the next page
assertions.rationale: ResultsToken is a Duende-specific pagination mechanism not discoverable from general knowledge.

assertions.assertion: Code uses RemoveSessionsAsync with RevokeConsents = false to preserve consent grants
assertions.rationale: An LLM with general Duende knowledge may know RemoveSessionsAsync exists but the specific flags are less certain. Giving benefit of doubt as the API is somewhat intuitive.

assertions.assertion: Code sets SendBackchannelLogoutNotification = true (explicitly or by relying on the default) in RemoveSessionsContext
assertions.rationale: The exact default values and property names of RemoveSessionsContext are Duende-specific details.

assertions.assertion: Response mentions preferring GetSessionsAsync over QuerySessionsAsync for simple session listing
assertions.rationale: This performance recommendation is specific to Duende docs and unlikely to be known without the skill.
